notes: Coverage is the last 7 days in Asia/Taipei.

Google-account-backed connector/API fallback remained frozen. The intern workbook tab opened in the in-app Browser with title '4月先博薪資彙整 - Google 試算表', but deeper visible account/metadata reads timed out; source modified_time therefore remains the last verified timestamp instead of a newly claimed value.
Items are deduplicated against the prior week by id and source URL; this week contains six new items.

highlights.title: Google workbook refresh is browser-blocked beyond tab title
highlights.why: The approved browser surface opened the intended Sheet URL, but DOM/account/metadata reads timed out twice, so the run preserved the previous verified workbook timestamp and avoided frozen Google connector fallback.
highlights.next: Treat this as google_browser_blocked for current Google metadata/download freshness; ask Kevin to keep the Sheet open and responsive before the next run if current modified_time proof is required.

highlights.title: Work-rhythm signal base expanded to 73 people
highlights.why: The read-only InfoCenter helper refreshed aggregate rhythm data from 500 events and increased the person count from 72 to 73 while keeping raw titles/comments out of the repo.
highlights.next: Use rhythm labels only for assignment timing and check-in preparation, not reimbursement or payroll evidence.

highlights.title: Frontier AI operations now need release-control tracking
highlights.why: Codex shipped a trace-log privacy fix, while Anthropic published Sonnet 5 and Fable 5 safeguard updates in the same week; the operational theme is controlled agent capability plus auditability.
highlights.next: Keep one weekly checklist item for model/tool release risk: privacy logs, approval gates, and which work may be delegated.

items.title: Google workbook freshness could not be re-proven through the browser
items.oneLine: The intended intern workbook opened in the in-app Browser, but account/context/modified-time reads timed out, so the automation kept the last verified source timestamp.
facts: The in-app Browser tab list showed the intended URL: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1X1bF9FIhqsOhVLl9nLfewoPuivfGKdvb/edit?gid=550857600#gid=550857600.
The visible tab title was '4月先博薪資彙整 - Google 試算表'.
Two deeper browser reads timed out and reset the control session, so no current visible account or Google modified_time was captured this run.
The automation did not use Google Drive, Sheets, Docs, or connector/API fallback because the 2026-06-26 freeze is still active.
yourTake: This is a valid stop condition for Google-backed freshness, not a reason to bypass the freeze.
The safest local outcome is a no-op intern rebuild using the last verified timestamp, plus an explicit blocker note.
actions: Before the next run, open the workbook in the in-app Browser and confirm it is responsive under egroup.kevin@gmail.com.
If exact source freshness matters this week, rerun only the browser workbook-export section after the browser is stable.

sources.label: Visible Codex in-app Browser - Google Sheets workbook tab

credibility.reason: Direct browser tab evidence for URL/title; metadata freshness intentionally not claimed.

followups: Recheck browser-visible account and workbook export path before using any current Google freshness claim.

items.title: InfoCenter work-rhythm refreshed with one more person in aggregate signals
items.oneLine: The read-only rhythm helper scanned 500 events and generated aggregate planning signals for 73 people without publishing raw event content.
facts: The helper wrote data/work-rhythm.internal.json and private raw evidence stayed under the workspace work folder.
This run reported scannedEvents=500, peopleCount=73, totalSignals=480, highConfidenceSignals=0.
The output remains aggregate-only: no raw event titles, comments, salary amounts, bank data, ID numbers, or full addresses.
yourTake: The new count is useful for assignment and support planning, but it is still a planning signal rather than an authority record.
Because high-confidence signals remain zero, use the page as a prompt for human check-in rather than automatic assignment routing.
actions: Use the work-rhythm page before weekly dispatch to spot who may need clearer check-in timing.
Keep reimbursement sheets and explicit work records as the source of truth for pay/work proof.

sources.label: InfoCenter event list - read-only configured helper

credibility.reason: Live helper read succeeded, but the published artifact intentionally contains aggregate rhythm evidence only.

followups: Check whether the 73-person output changes the weekly assignment/support list.

items.title: Codex CLI shipped a trace-log privacy fix
items.oneLine: Codex CLI 0.142.5 prevents full Responses WebSocket request payloads from being written to trace logs.
facts: OpenAI's Codex changelog lists Codex CLI 0.142.5 on 2026-07-01.
The release note identifies a bug fix that prevents full Responses WebSocket request payloads from being written to trace logs.
The 2026-06-29 and 2026-06-26 CLI releases were maintenance-only/no user-facing change entries.
yourTake: For Kevin's local automation layer, this matters because trace/log boundaries are part of privacy hygiene when prompts may mention private workbooks, account surfaces, or internal data.
It is not a feature to chase immediately, but it belongs in the weekly tool-safety watchlist.
actions: When upgrading Codex CLI next, verify the installed version and keep sensitive payload logging out of any shared artifact.
Add log-scrubbing to future automation debugging notes when browser/account/private workbook context is involved.
followups: Check local Codex CLI version during the next maintenance window before making any upgrade recommendation.

items.title: Anthropic paired broader agent access with Fable 5 safeguard updates
items.oneLine: Anthropic's newsroom shows Sonnet 5, Fable 5 redeployment, and cyber-safeguard details clustered around June 30 to July 2.
facts: The same newsroom window included 'Redeploying Fable 5' on 2026-06-30 and more Fable 5 cyber-safeguard details on 2026-07-02.
The pattern is agent capability becoming broader while governance and jailbreak severity scoring become more explicit.
yourTake: The practical lesson for Kevin's workflows is not model hype; it is that agentic delegation needs a safety/control checklist next to the capability checklist.
This can inform how FamilyFin or internal automation distinguishes low-risk drafting, browser observation, and account-backed operation.
actions: Draft a small 'agent delegation risk' checklist: source mode, private data exposure, external action, and rollback path.
Use the checklist before assigning repetitive account-backed tasks to any model/tool.

credibility.reason: Primary Anthropic newsroom listing with dated releases.

followups: Revisit if Anthropic publishes the detailed jailbreak framework as a reusable scorecard.

items.title: MOHW social-work capacity signal shifted to administrative hiring
items.oneLine: The social-work radar this week is less a policy headline and more a staffing/administrative-capacity watch point.
facts: MOHW's employment list shows a 2026-06-29 research assistant opening in the Department of Social Assistance and Social Work, closing 2026-07-06.
The role includes public fundraising, fundraising management-system work, and social assistance/relief related tasks.
The same list also shows late-June social-assistance/social-work office openings and deadlines extending into early July.
yourTake: For workforce planning, this suggests the official system still needs administrative capacity around relief, fundraising, and case-support infrastructure.
It is a useful background signal for partner conversations about why social-work support requires operational staff, not only frontline professionals.
actions: Keep a July 6 follow-up to see whether the opening closes normally or reappears.
Translate this into one partner-facing talking point about back-office load behind social-service delivery.

sources.label: MOHW research assistant opening - Social Assistance and Social Work

followups: Check the July 6 deadline and whether a selection result appears.

items.title: FSC financial education outputs remain resource-map material
items.oneLine: FSC surfaces continue to point toward curated financial-education resources and measurable inclusion targets, useful for FamilyFin content planning.
facts: FSC's financial knowledge page lists official and affiliated resources such as MoneyWise, financial mini-encyclopedia, risk/insurance education, training courses, and dispute/consumer education materials.
FSC's inclusive-finance results page says 2026 indicators include expanded financial-knowledge outreach for groups such as new residents and women.
A recent FSC law/news listing also surfaced '金融教育成果專刊' around 2026-06-30.
yourTake: The immediate content opportunity is a resource map: where to learn, calculate, verify terms, and ask for consumer-dispute help.
This is better than a generic financial-literacy article because it gives users official next stops.
actions: Build one FamilyFin draft outline that maps user needs to official FSC-linked resources.
Track whether the 2026 financial-education results publication exposes reusable case examples.

credibility.reason: Primary FSC pages, but the results-publication detail needs a dedicated follow-up if it will be quoted.

followups: Find the downloadable financial-education results publication and extract reusable case categories.
